name: Defense & Aerospace Sector
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **ITA +3.7% TO $223.42 BUT AEROSPACE-LED, NOT DEFENSE-LED — IMPORTANT ATTRIBUTION FLAG.** ITA's top holdings are GE Aerospace (19.4%), RTX (15.1%), Boeing (10.3%) — together >40% of the ETF. Boeing 737/787 commercial backlog benefits from peace-track resolution (passenger demand, Gulf carrier orders); GE Aerospace order book at $190B with $1B US-ops investment + 5,000 hire announcement. **Lockheed Martin (pure-play defense, missile systems) only +1.0% — and -18.1% on the month — confirming peace-track is BEARISH for pure defense.** The +3.7% ITA print MASKS the underlying split: aerospace ripping, defense flat-to-up modestly. **Critical reading guidance: if you read 'Defense ETF +3.7%' as 'war is back on,' you are wrong.** The signal is the OPPOSITE — peace track tailwind for commercial aero outweighs defense weakness. Lockheed's -18.1% 1M is the actual defense signal: war-premium has unwound substantially. Crisis-linked attribution: ~20% (peace-track tailwind for commercial aero); ~80% Boeing+GE recovery. Reversibility: if MOU signs, ITA rotation continues toward commercial-aero leadership; LMT continues to bleed. If Iran rejects, LMT/RTX bounce +5-8%, Boeing/GE give back small portion. Bifurcation thesis structurally validated.

active_triggers.condition: ITA tests $208 sustained on Branch 3 deepening (3+ sessions)
